prose:
**Fukui Reactivity Analysis of Aniline — DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier standard), PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.** The neutral parent molecule and its N±1 ionized states (cation/anion, both doublets) were computed at B3LYP/def2-TZVP via PySCF RKS/UKS. Mulliken partial charges were extracted for each state, and per-atom Fukui indices were derived: f⁺ (electrophilicity, sensitivity to electron-accepting) from the cation; f⁻ (nucleophilicity, sensitivity to electron-donating) from the anion; f⁰ (ambiphilicity) as the average; and the dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) to identify dominant character. All three electronic states converged to SCF convergence; charge conservation checks confirmed sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. Integrity status: trustworthy. 

**Most Electrophilic Site:** Carbon at position 3 (ortho to the amino group), f⁺ = 2.2975867 (Fukui index, dimensionless).

**Most Nucleophilic Site:** Nitrogen (amino group, position 1), f⁻ = 0.20452359 (Fukui index, dimensionless).

**Full per-atom Fukui indices and dual descriptors:** reported above in tabular form; the ortho-carbons show strong electrophilicity (dual descriptor ≈ 2.23, positive), while the ipso-carbon shows nucleophilicity (dual descriptor ≈ −1.90, negative). The amino nitrogen is modestly nucleophilic (f⁻ ≈ 0.20).

Command: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz --accept-defaults --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmptyvkfmnc/live.json`

No warnings were reported by the engine. Geometry: user-supplied aniline.xyz, 14 atoms (1 N, 6 C, 7 H), used as-is at the single-point fukui level (no geometry optimization). Output JSON written to the engine's scratch directory.</prose>
<parameter name="value">2.2975867
